SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.valve

Enum Class ValveSeatLeakageClass

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<ValveSeatLeakageClass>
neqsim.process.equipment.valve.ValveSeatLeakageClass
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<ValveSeatLeakageClass>, Constable
public enum ValveSeatLeakageClass extends Enum<ValveSeatLeakageClass>
Qualitative and, where the underlying standard defines a closed-form expression, quantitative valve seat-leakage tightness classification.

Covers the EN 12266-1 leakage rates A through G (used widely for on/off and isolation valves, e.g. ESDV/HIPPS block valves) and the ANSI/FCI 70-2 control-valve seat-leakage classes II through VI (used for throttling control valves). The enum constants carry only a qualitative description and an "essentially zero leakage" flag; the exact numeric acceptance criteria for EN 12266-1 vary with valve size and test medium and must be taken from the standard itself.

For ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class V, which defines a closed-form liquid leak-rate relation, this class also provides estimateFciClassVLeakRate(double, double) as an illustrative screening helper.

This classification is intended for engineering screening (e.g. ranking candidate valve tightness requirements during a safety study) and is not a substitute for a manufacturer seat leakage test certificate.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Enum.EnumDesc<E extends Enum<E>>

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    EN_12266_RATE_A
    EN 12266-1 Rate A: least stringent metal-seated leakage rate.
    EN_12266_RATE_B
    EN 12266-1 Rate B.
    EN_12266_RATE_C
    EN 12266-1 Rate C.
    EN_12266_RATE_D
    EN 12266-1 Rate D.
    EN_12266_RATE_E
    EN 12266-1 Rate E.
    EN_12266_RATE_F
    EN 12266-1 Rate F.
    EN_12266_RATE_G
    EN 12266-1 Rate G: most stringent, essentially bubble-tight metal seat.
    FCI_70_2_CLASS_II
    ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class II: 0.5% of rated capacity.
    FCI_70_2_CLASS_III
    ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class III: 0.1% of rated capacity.
    FCI_70_2_CLASS_IV
    ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class IV: 0.01% of rated capacity (typical default for control valves).
    FCI_70_2_CLASS_V
    ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class V: leak rate scales with port diameter and differential pressure.
    FCI_70_2_CLASS_VI
    ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class VI: resilient (soft) seat, essentially zero liquid leakage.

      public static ValveSeatLeakageClass valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

      public String getStandardDesignation()

      Returns:
      the standard designation, e.g. "ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class IV"

      public String getDescription()
      Gets a qualitative description of this leakage rate/class.
      Returns:
      the description text

    • isEssentiallyZeroLeakage

      public boolean isEssentiallyZeroLeakage()
      Indicates whether this rate/class is considered essentially zero leakage for screening purposes.
      Returns:
      true if leakage is considered essentially zero, false otherwise

    • estimateFciClassVLeakRate

      public static double estimateFciClassVLeakRate(double diffPressurePa, double portDiameterM)
      Estimates the illustrative ANSI/FCI 70-2 Class V liquid seat-leakage rate for a given differential pressure and port diameter.

      Implements the standard correlation Q [mL/min] = 5e-4 * d [inch] * dP [psi], where the test medium is water. This formula is only valid near the valve's rated test differential pressure and should not be extrapolated to extreme overpressure scenarios without engineering judgement; it is intended for screening and comparison purposes only, not as a certified leakage guarantee.

      Parameters:
      diffPressurePa - the differential pressure across the closed valve seat, in Pa, must be positive
      portDiameterM - the valve port (seat) diameter, in meters, must be positive
      Returns:
      the estimated Class V liquid leak rate, in mL/min
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if diffPressurePa or portDiameterM is not positive
